seo_description: Current time in Tallinn, Estonia. Eastern European Time — EET (UTC+2) in winter, EEST (UTC+3) during DST. Live clock and time differences for Tallinn.

faq.question: What time is it in Tallinn right now?
faq.answer: Tallinn, Estonia uses Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) in winter and Eastern European Summer Time (EEST, UTC+3) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Europe/Tallinn. Estonia follows the EU DST schedule, with clocks springing forward on the last Sunday in March and falling back on the last Sunday in October. The live clock above shows the current Tallinn time.

faq.question: What time zone is Tallinn in?
faq.answer: Tallinn, Estonia is in the Eastern European Time Zone. It uses Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) in winter and Eastern European Summer Time (EEST, UTC+3) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Europe/Tallinn. Estonia is an EU member state and follows EU DST rules. Tallinn shares its time zone with Riga (Latvia), Vilnius (Lithuania), Helsinki (Finland), and Kyiv (Ukraine).

faq.question: Does Tallinn observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Tallinn, Estonia observes Daylight Saving Time following the EU schedule. Clocks spring forward on the last Sunday in March and fall back on the last Sunday in October. Estonia is one of the EU countries that has voted to end DST, but EU-wide implementation has been postponed indefinitely.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tallinn and London?
faq.answer: Tallinn (EET, UTC+2) is 2 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer (BST, UTC+1) and Estonian summer (EEST, UTC+3), Tallinn is still 2 hours ahead. Both countries change clocks on the same EU schedule.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tallinn and Helsinki?
faq.answer: Tallinn and Helsinki are in the same time zone — Eastern European Time (EET/EEST, UTC+2/+3). They always share the exact same time. Helsinki and Tallinn are separated by the Gulf of Finland — ferries connect them in under 2 hours, making it a frequent business and leisure route.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Tallinn?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Tallinn are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM EET/EEST, Monday through Friday. Tallinn is Estonia's capital and one of the most digitally advanced cities in the world — Estonia was the first country to allow internet voting in national elections. Home to Skype (founded here), Transferwise/Wise, and many other tech startups. The Tallinn Stock Exchange is part of the Nasdaq Baltic market. Tallinn's medieval Old Town is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
